title: digitalis purpurea subsp. carpetana
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. carpetana is a subspecies of the foxglove plant, characterized by its compact, bushy habit and pale purple flowers. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its digitalis glycosides, which have been found to have a positive inotropic effect on the heart, increasing its contractility and slowing heart rate. The medicinal properties of Digitalis purpurea subsp. carpetana include its ability to treat heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of this plant include dried leaves and flowers, which can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its potential toxicity.
